name: Fungal leaf spot / blight
organicTreatment: Remove heavily infected leaves and keep them away from compost used on the same crop.
Improve spacing and airflow; avoid evening overhead irrigation.
Use locally recommended Trichoderma-enriched compost or bio-fungicide guidance from KVK.
prevention: Use clean seed and rotate crops.

name: Aphids or sucking pest attack
organicTreatment: Spray plain water on early clusters to dislodge pests.
Encourage ladybird beetles and avoid broad-spectrum chemicals.
Use neem-based botanical spray only at locally recommended dilution and cool-hour timing.
prevention: Keep flowering strips or habitat for beneficial insects.

name: Nutrient or water stress
organicTreatment: Check soil moisture at root depth before irrigation.
Apply mature compost or jeevamrit as locally practiced; avoid over-application.
Mulch to reduce heat and moisture stress.
prevention: Maintain organic matter with compost and residue mulch.

name: Possible onion downy mildew
organicTreatment: Remove badly affected leaves and improve field ventilation.
Avoid overhead watering and irrigate early morning.
Confirm with KVK before any treatment because onion foliar disease spreads quickly.
prevention: Rotate away from onion family crops.
